description: Prioritized aggressive ideas for a 5090 discovery loop. Start from a stable near-frontier seed, isolate one primary novelty at a time, and only spend repeated attempts on families with evidence.
variant_rule_default: Each attempt should test one primary novelty on top of a stable carrier anchor. Do not bundle topology, quantization, schedule, and module-family changes into one 5-minute proxy unless the blueprint explicitly requires it.

campaign_default_regime.seq_len: 768 stable seed; only push to 1024-1536 when throughput holds
campaign_default_regime.batch_tokens: 131072 stable seed; change only with explicit reason

ideas.title: Stable Frontier Seed + One Local Novelty
ideas.goal: Use the frontier seed carrier and test one train.py-local novelty at a time: activation family, partial RoPE, residual-scale initialization, or a clip-override support change.
forbidden_refinements: changing carrier partition and quantization groups in the same run
dropping back to the compact recurrent baseline
adding a new module family on the same run as the local knob
hints: Keep the seed carrier stable and interpretable.
Use one primary novelty and at most one obvious support change.
These are high-EV discovery runs because they preserve throughput while probing transferable ideas.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Leaky-ReLU-Squared Seed
attempt_blueprints.summary: Keep the near-frontier seed carrier fixed and switch the MLP activation family to leaky-ReLU-squared, with no other material topology change.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Partial-RoPE Seed
attempt_blueprints.summary: Keep the same carrier and test a partial-RoPE fraction below 1.0, without also changing the quantization story.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Residual-Scale Initialization Seed
attempt_blueprints.summary: Keep the same carrier and test a non-default residual-scale initialization while leaving activation and quantization otherwise familiar.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: Best Local Knob + Clip Support
attempt_blueprints.summary: Take the strongest still-plausible local knob from the previous seed attempts and pair it with one coarse clip-percentile support change, still without a new module family.

ideas.title: Late Selective Quantization on a Stable Carrier
ideas.goal: Test PTQ and late coarse-group QAT on a stable near-frontier carrier instead of using many precision micro-carve-outs or blanket INT4 experiments.
forbidden_refinements: single-tensor float exemptions on an otherwise unchanged branch
INT4-only MLP sweeps as the main story
changing the carrier and local module family in the same attempt
hints: Think in groups: embeddings/head, middle MLPs, late attention, final MLPs.
If a run gets close but is invalid, the next attempt should be a budget repair, not a new story.
Post-quant checkpoint choice is part of the hypothesis, not an afterthought.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: PTQ Anchor
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use the stable carrier with no train-time QAT and denser warmdown checkpoints so the attempt honestly tests PTQ plus checkpoint choice.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Middle-MLP Late QAT
attempt_blueprints.summary: Keep the carrier fixed and turn on late QAT only for middle MLP groups, while keeping embeddings, head, and final MLPs safer.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Late-Attention + Middle-MLP QAT
attempt_blueprints.summary: Keep the same carrier and test one broader but still coarse late-QAT grouping: late attention plus middle MLPs only.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: Post-Quant Selector
attempt_blueprints.summary: Keep the carrier stable and make the main novelty the post-quant selection rule: checkpoint choice, tiny soup, or clip-override ranking.

ideas.title: Checkpoint Choice and Post-Quant Soup
ideas.goal: Treat checkpoint choice as a first-class search variable and use post-quant quality, not float loss alone, as the ranking signal.
forbidden_refinements: changing carrier topology at the same time
quant-group sweeps plus checkpoint-soup changes in one run
new module-family additions
hints: This is a strong follow-on to a stable carrier because it preserves attribution.
A small soup or a different warmdown cadence is enough for one run.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Dense Warmdown Selection
attempt_blueprints.summary: Keep the stable carrier and use denser warmdown checkpoint saves so the run can rank checkpoints by post-quant quality.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Tiny Post-Quant Soup
attempt_blueprints.summary: Keep the stable carrier and test a tiny soup of adjacent late checkpoints instead of relying on a single float winner.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Clip-Aware Selector
attempt_blueprints.summary: Keep the stable carrier and make the main novelty a post-quant selector that sweeps a small number of coarse clip settings before final ranking.

ideas.title: Low-Rank Q Reallocation
ideas.goal: Use low-rank Q to buy one obvious macro spend at a time: one more unique layer, one cleaner width increase, or one cautious context increase.
forbidden_refinements: low-rank Q without a visible spend of the savings
spending the savings on multiple unrelated changes at once
using low-rank Q only to make an already weak local-module branch more complicated
hints: Recent evidence suggests low-rank-Q can look good but often breaks the byte cap; prefer one budget repair or one single spend per run.
Use the stable carrier as the anchor whenever possible.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: One Extra Unique Layer
attempt_blueprints.summary: Apply low-rank Q and spend the savings on exactly one additional unique tail layer, keeping the rest of the carrier close to the stable seed.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Width Spend
attempt_blueprints.summary: Apply low-rank Q and spend the savings on one cleaner MLP-width increase on the stable carrier, without adding a new module family.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: 1024 Context Spend
attempt_blueprints.summary: Apply low-rank Q and spend the savings on a cautious move to seq_len 1024, keeping the rest of the carrier simple enough that the context hypothesis is interpretable.

attempt_blueprints.label: D
attempt_blueprints.title: Budget Repair
attempt_blueprints.summary: If a previous low-rank-Q branch looked good but was invalid, use this attempt for a strict budget repair rather than another new mechanism.

ideas.title: Localized Canon-Style Inserts
ideas.goal: Test a single localized Canon-style insert on an otherwise stable carrier, because this family showed the best recent creativity-to-score ratio among the module-writing ideas.
forbidden_refinements: multiple local-module families in one run
Canon insert plus a large carrier repartition
Canon insert plus a new quantization story on the same run
hints: One insert location per run is enough.
If the branch is alive but invalid, the next attempt should be a repair or placement simplification.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Bottom Insert
attempt_blueprints.summary: Add one small Canon-style insert low in the stack while keeping the carrier and quantization story otherwise stable.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Pre-Final Insert
attempt_blueprints.summary: Move the same Canon-style idea to a pre-final placement, without also changing carrier depth or QAT grouping.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Placement Repair
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use the strongest still-plausible Canon attempt but simplify placement or width so the run is cleaner and easier to keep under budget.

ideas.title: XSA or Top-Layer Cache
ideas.goal: Try one clean XSA or cache mechanism at a time, late in the campaign, after the higher-EV stable-carrier and post-quant stories have been tested.
forbidden_refinements: combining XSA and cache and TTT in one run
using XSA as a carrier-only excuse without a real mechanism
raising context aggressively and changing quantization at the same time
hints: Use only the final 2-3 layers if you add a real mechanism.
Keep the rest of the carrier stable and explainable.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Top-Tail XSA
attempt_blueprints.summary: Add real XSA logic only to the final 2-3 layers of a stable carrier, with no extra cache or TTT stack.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Lightweight Prefix Cache
attempt_blueprints.summary: Use a lightweight top-layer prefix cache or summary cache only in the upper tail, still keeping the rest of the carrier stable.

attempt_blueprints.label: C
attempt_blueprints.title: Context-Limited Mechanism Test
attempt_blueprints.summary: Test the strongest still-plausible XSA or cache idea but keep context limited to a cautious increase so the run stays interpretable and not obviously undertrained.

ideas.title: Small Local-Token Module
ideas.goal: Reserve one or two late low-priority attempts for truly small local-token modules that do not explode the proxy with many simultaneous changes.
forbidden_refinements: large local modules plus carrier repartition in one run
combining a new local module with an INT4 sweep
multiple local-token families in one attempt
hints: Recent evidence says heavyweight local modules are low EV in this proxy.
If you test one, keep it tiny and well-isolated.

attempt_blueprints.label: A
attempt_blueprints.title: Factorized Bigram
attempt_blueprints.summary: Add one very small factorized-bigram style local feature on an otherwise stable carrier.

attempt_blueprints.label: B
attempt_blueprints.title: Hashed Tail Local Feature
attempt_blueprints.summary: Try one simple hashed-tail local token feature as the main novelty, still without a large carrier or quantization rewrite.
